scope: RA3: native W==D with AMD_ISA_REG_ACCUM=1 (pinned loop-carried accumulators)

correctness.hidden_fallback: no HIP/LLVM block tile, no owned tile in candidate route

root_cause: the fixed pin reservation v240..v255 forces the kernel descriptor to allocate VGPRs up to the highest pinned reg used: native tile VGPR 56 -> 248 (granulated). At ctx512 (few split-workgroups) occupancy is not binding, so the per-token LDS-op reduction (RA2: DS 31->9) yields +5.3%. At ctx4096 (~43 splits, many workgroups) occupancy IS binding, and VGPR=248 collapses waves/CU -> -17.9%.
ra2_lds_reduction_holds: DS load/store 31->9 (lds_accum_stage), token-correct -- the accumulator state IS off LDS; the regression is a SECONDARY VGPR-count side effect, not a correctness or LDS failure.
secondary_issue: elf still sizes the (now-unused) accumulator LDS (group_segment 12288 unchanged) because the DEFINE_REG stays reachable for ordering -> LDS occupancy not reclaimed either.
fix_direction: occupancy-aware pin placement: instead of a FIXED high base (v240), renumber pinned accumulators CONTIGUOUS with the virtual high-water mark (virtual_max+1 .. +N) in a post-regalloc pass, so the descriptor VGPR count = virtual_max + n_pins (e.g. ~56+12=68) instead of 248; and drop the pinned DEFINE_REG from elf group-segment sizing. Both are refinements to the RA1 reservation model, deferred (out of the staged RA0-RA3 design which used a fixed reserved range).
disposition: feature is CORRECT and RA0/RA1/RA2 PASS; default AMD_ISA_REG_ACCUM=0 unchanged. NOT promotable as-is due to the ctx4096 VGPR-inflation regression. Net across ctx is negative -> do not enable by default until the contiguous-placement refinement lands.
